a washing off, i.e. (ceremonially) ablution, (morally) expiation
a bench (literally or figuratively)
to lie down to rest, i.e. (by implication) to fall asleep (literally or figuratively)
and (to sit; akin to the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος)); to sit down; figuratively, to remain, reside
to seat down, i.e. set (figuratively, appoint); intransitively, to sit (down); figuratively, to settle (hover, dwell)
just (or inasmuch) as, that
kjv: according to, (according, even) as, how, when

en: and, also, even, so then, too, etc.; often used in connection (or composition) with other particles or small words
kjv: and, also, both, but, even, for, if, or, so, that, then, therefore, when, yet

en: an occasion, i.e. set or proper time
kjv: X always, opportunity, (convenient, due) season, (due, short, while) time, a while

en: Cæsar, a title of the Roman emperor
Cæsaria, the name of two places in Palestine
likewise in that place
likewise from that place (or time)
likewise that (or those)
kjv: and him (other, them), even he, him also, them (also), (and) they

en: to revile
to be a bad-doer, i.e. (objectively) to injure, or (genitive) to sin
worthless (intrinsically, such; whereas G4190 (πονηρός) properly refers to effects), i.e. (subjectively) depraved, or (objectively) injurious
kjv: bad, evil, harm, ill, noisome, wicked

en: badly (physically or morally)
kjv: amiss, diseased, evil, grievously, miserably, sick, sore

en: a reed (the plant or its stem, or that of a similar plant); by implication, a pen
to "call" (properly, aloud, but used in a variety of applications, directly or otherwise)

kjv: X better, fair, good(-ly), honest, meet, well, worthy

en: well (usually morally)
a "camel"
and (or even) if
kjv: and (also) if (so much as), if but, at the least, though, yet

en: zealous; Cananites, an epithet
kjv: Canaanite (by mistake for a derivative from G5477 (Χαναάν))

en: Capernaum (i.e. Caphanachum), a place in Palestine
the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle
fruit (as plucked), literally or figuratively
to be fertile (literally or figuratively)
(prepositionally) down (in place or time), in varied relations (according to the case (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined)
kjv: about, according as (to), after, against, (when they were) X alone, among, and, X apart, (even, like) as (concerning, pertaining to touching), X aside, at, before, beyond, by, to the charge of, (charita-)bly, concerning, + covered, (dai-)ly, down, every, (+ far more) exceeding, X more excellent, for, from … to, godly, in(-asmuch, divers, every, -to, respect of), … by, after the manner of, + by any means, beyond (out of) measure, X mightily, more, X natural, of (up-)on (X part), out (of every), over against, (+ your) X own, + particularly, so, through(-oughout, -oughout every), thus, (un-)to(-gether, -ward), X uttermost, where(-by), with

en: to descend (literally or figuratively)
to impose upon
to laugh down, i.e. deride
to hunt down, i.e. search for
to lie down, i.e. (by implication) be sick; specially, to recline at a meal
to break down, i.e. divide
to chop down, i.e. mangle
to judge against, i.e. sentence
to lord against, i.e. control, subjugate
kjv: exercise dominion over (lordship), be lord over, overcome

en: to take eagerly, i.e. seize, possess, etc. (literally or figuratively)
kjv: apprehend, attain, come upon, comprehend, find, obtain, perceive, (over-)take

en: to leave down, i.e. behind; by implication, to abandon, have remaining
properly, a dissolution (breaking up of a journey), i.e. (by implication) a lodging-place
to loosen down (disintegrate), i.e. (by implication) to demolish (literally or figuratively); specially (compare G2646 (κατάλυμα)) to halt for the night
kjv: destroy, dissolve, be guest, lodge, come to nought, overthrow, throw down

en: to testify against
something spread thoroughly, i.e. (specially) the door screen (to the Most Holy Place) in the Jewish Temple
to execrate; by analogy, to doom
to complete thoroughly, i.e. repair (literally or figuratively) or adjust
kjv: fit, frame, mend, (make) perfect(-ly join together), prepare, restore

en: to prepare thoroughly (properly, by external equipment; whereas G2090 (ἑτοιμάζω) refers rather to internal fitness); by implication, to construct, create
to camp down, i.e. haunt; figuratively, to remain
to turn upside down, i.e. upset
to place down, i.e. deposit (literally or figuratively)
to kiss earnestly
to pour down (out)
directly opposite
to have (wield) full privilege over
to eat down, i.e. devour (literally or figuratively)
to be a plaintiff, i.e. to charge with some offence
residence (properly, the act; but by implication, concretely, the mansion)
downwards
to burn
empty (literally or figuratively)
a centurion, i.e. captain of one hundred soldiers
an earthenware vessel, i.e. jar
to gain (literally or figuratively)

the head (as the part most readily taken hold of), literally or figuratively
properly, an enrollment ("census"), i.e. (by implication) a tax
to herald (as a public crier), especially divine truth (the gospel)
to stir (transitively), literally or figuratively
a twig or bough (as if broken off)
to sob, i.e. wail aloud (whereas 1145 is rather to cry silently)
a piece (bit)
to break (specially, of bread)
to filch
to be an heir to (literally or figuratively)
heirship, i.e. (concretely) a patrimony or (genitive case) a possession
a sharer by lot, i.e. inheritor (literally or figuratively); by implication, a possessor
a die (for drawing chances); by implication, a portion (as if so secured); by extension, an acquisition (especially a patrimony, figuratively)
a couch (for sleep, sickness, sitting or eating)
stealing
a quadrans, i.e. the fourth part of an as
a cavity, i.e. (especially) the abdomen; by implication, the matrix; figuratively, the heart
common, i.e. (literally) shared by all or several, or (ceremonially) profane
